questions.question: Why are PSO and ACO a natural fit for the LLM era?
options: They run natively on GPUs

Prompts are discrete strings you cannot backprop through — bio-inspired optimization is gradient-free, population-based, and cheap per evaluation, exactly the regime prompt and routing optimization live in
They were invented by LLM researchers
They eliminate the need for evals
questions.explanation: Manual prompt tweaking doesn't scale and RL needs reward plumbing and rollouts. PSO handles continuous-ish search (prompt/parameter space); ACO handles path selection (which agent for which task type).

questions.question: In LMPSO, what plays the role of a particle's velocity?
options: A learning-rate float
A prompt describing how to modify the current output toward the personal/global best — the LLM generates the next candidate from it; "inertia" is a prompt like "make small incremental changes"
The token-generation speed
A gradient vector
questions.explanation: PSO's update rule is reinterpreted in language space. It works when outputs are structured and fitness is automatic (test runs, arithmetic checks) with small populations — and fails when fitness needs human review per iteration.

questions.question: What is Model Swarms' key insight, and its reported result?
options: Bigger swarms always beat smaller ones

Expert LLMs are nearby on a shared parameter manifold (adapters, LoRA deltas), so PSO on that low-dimensional subspace is cheap — 13.3% average gain over 12 baselines on 9 datasets with just 200 instances
Swarms only work with identical models
Weight averaging beats all fine-tuning
questions.explanation: PSO moves from the output layer to the model layer: each particle IS an expert's parameters, and the swarm moves them gradient-free toward the collective best.

questions.question: Why does AMRO-S gate pheromone updates behind quality checks?
options: To save database writes
Without the gate, fast-but-wrong agents accrue pheromone and the router locks in on bad routes — quality-gated asynchronous updates decouple inference from learning
Quality gates are an EU AI Act requirement
To make pheromones decay faster
questions.explanation: ACO-style routing strengthens agent-task routes that produce good outputs (4.7x speedup, human-readable pheromone evidence). The gate is the integrity condition — the same evaluator-rigor theme from the evolutionary-coding lessons.

questions.question: When is PSO/ACO the WRONG tool for an LLM problem?
options: Whenever a GPU is available

When fitness requires human review per iteration (cost explodes), when outputs are unstructured and unevaluable, or when the search space has a trainable gradient path that RL/fine-tuning exploits better
When the population exceeds 5 particles
Never — they always apply
questions.explanation: These are evaluator-bound methods: automatic, cheap fitness is the precondition. The lesson's framing throughout — population search amplifies whatever the evaluator measures, good or bad.
